message: The gated integration test TestIntegration_Agy skips unless AGY_MODEL is set, but the contract's validation command_004 (DEBATE_EXEC_INTEGRATION=1 go test -count=1 -tags exec_integration ./internal/backend/...) does not set AGY_MODEL. The test therefore skips at integration_test.go:21-24 and invokes no agy subprocess; the gate reports 'ok' only because a skipped test passes. Acceptance criterion 5 claims this command 'exercises the real agy --print invocation end-to-end', but no subprocess ran, so the non-interactive --print behavior (the whole point of the change) is never validated by the gate. The unit tests use a fake runner and assert only argv strings, so they cannot prove --print actually prevents the interactive hang.
trigger: Gate/CI runs command_004 without AGY_MODEL set (the default invocation); TestIntegration_Agy skips and no agy subprocess is spawned, so --print non-interactive behavior is unverified.
fix_direction: Set AGY_MODEL in the command_004 validation invocation (and ensure agy is available in the gate environment) so the integration test actually runs, OR explicitly document that command_004 is a manual-only check and that the gate does not validate the --print non-interactive path; do not assert command_004 validates --print while it silently skips.
evidence: gate command_004 = 'DEBATE_EXEC_INTEGRATION=1 go test -count=1 -tags exec_integration ./internal/backend/...' (no AGY_MODEL). integration_test.go:21-24: `model := os.Getenv("AGY_MODEL"); if model == "" { t.Skip(...) }`. gate/validation/command_004/stdout.log shows 'ok github.com/heurema/debate/internal/backend/exec' with no -v, consistent with a skipped test. exec_test.go TestCmd_Default/TestCmd_Override use fakeRunner and assert only rec.args strings, never real non-interactive behavior.

message: Acceptance criterion 5 claims the gated integration test 'exercises the real agy --print invocation end-to-end ... serving as the runnable validation of the --print non-interactive behavior', but the gate never actually ran it. TestIntegration_Agy requires AGY_MODEL to be set (integration_test.go:21-24), while the contract's validation command (command_004) sets only DEBATE_EXEC_INTEGRATION=1. The test therefore skipped: command_004 stdout shows 'ok github.com/heurema/debate/internal/backend/exec 0.520s' with duration_ms=699 — far too fast for a real LLM CLI call. The --print no-hang behavior, which is the entire purpose of this change, has no automated validation; correctness rests solely on the contract's stated assumption about agy 1.0.11.
trigger: When the gate runs command_004 with DEBATE_EXEC_INTEGRATION=1 but without AGY_MODEL set (and/or without agy installed), TestIntegration_Agy hits the AGY_MODEL skip guard and never spawns agy.
fix_direction: Either set AGY_MODEL (and ensure agy is installed) in the integration validation command so the test actually invokes real agy --print, or reword acceptance criterion 5 / the integration test docs to state the end-to-end --print check must be run manually and is not covered by the automated gate.
evidence: integration_test.go:21-24 skips when AGY_MODEL is empty; the gate's command_004 command string is 'DEBATE_EXEC_INTEGRATION=1 go test -count=1 -tags exec_integration ./internal/backend/...' (no AGY_MODEL); command_004 stdout.log = 'ok github.com/heurema/debate/internal/backend/exec 0.520s' and result.json duration_ms=699, consistent with a skip rather than a real agy invocation.

message: Acceptance criterion #5 claims the gated integration test (TestIntegration_Agy) is the runnable validation of the agy --print non-interactive (no-hang) behavior, but the contract's validation command (command_004) only sets DEBATE_EXEC_INTEGRATION=1 and not AGY_MODEL. TestIntegration_Agy skips at integration_test.go:23 when AGY_MODEL is empty, so the real --print invocation is never exercised by the gate. The gate's command_004 finished in 0.52s with 'ok' and no real-agy response — i.e. it skipped. The only automated coverage of the change is the unit assertion that '--print' appears in argv (TestCmd_Default/TestCmd_Override); the actual 'does not hang against real agy' behavior is unverified by any command the gate runs.
trigger: Gate runs validation command_004 without AGY_MODEL in the environment; TestIntegration_Agy hits the AGY_MODEL skip and never invokes real agy.
fix_direction: Make the integration test actually run in the gate that claims to validate --print: either supply AGY_MODEL (and an available agy CLI) in the command_004 environment, or add a deterministic local agy stub (build-tagged, no network) that exercises the --print/stdin/exit path so a regression in the non-interactive invocation is caught without external credentials.
evidence: integration_test.go:21-24 skips unless AGY_MODEL is set; contract.json:30 validation command is 'DEBATE_EXEC_INTEGRATION=1 go test -count=1 -tags exec_integration ./internal/backend/...' with no AGY_MODEL; gate command_004 result.json shows exit 0, duration_ms 699, stdout 'ok ... exec 0.520s' (too fast for a real LLM call, no t.Logf response), confirming a skip. draft-proposal.md:41 acknowledges 'real integration validation may skip unless ... AGY_MODEL ... provided' and draft-proposal.md:36 originally included AGY_MODEL=$AGY_MODEL, which the final contract dropped.
